conversation.message: How have you been feeling lately?
Honestly, I’ve been really down. I try to focus on the day ahead, but the fatigue just wears me out.
You mentioned the fatigue wears you out—how has that been affecting your day-to-day routine?
It makes everything a chore. I used to enjoy simple things like walking the dog, but now it feels like climbing a mountain.
You said walking the dog used to be enjoyable—what else has started to feel like that?
Cooking. I used to love trying new recipes, but now it just feels like too much effort. I find myself ordering takeout or just eating cereal because it’s easier.
When you think about the future, what comes to mind?
Nothing. I look ahead and see nothing but more of this. I don’t know what the future holds, and that scares me.
You mentioned that things feel pointless - when did that start feeling this strong?
After the divorce. I used to have a reason to keep going, but now... I just feel like I’m just going through the motions.
When you say nothing matters, what parts of life feel most empty lately?
Everything. My job, my apartment, even my friendships. It’s all just... empty.
How do you see the next few weeks unfolding for you?
I don't know. I just keep going because I have to, but I don't see a way out of this.
How much of this feels like giving up versus feeling exhausted?
Mostly exhausted. I don't think I'm giving up, I just... I don't have the energy to pretend everything is fine anymore.
Do you feel like people would be better off without you, or is it more that you feel stuck?
I don't think anyone would miss me if I wasn't here. I'm just... invisible now.
You mentioned feeling invisible—has that made it harder to focus on things you need to do?
Yeah. I used to be the one who kept things moving, but now I feel like I'm just watching life happen from the outside.
When you feel like you're just watching from the outside, does that make you feel more on edge or restless?
It makes me want to scream. I feel like I'm just waiting for something to happen, but nothing ever does.
